concept: Outage / Period of Unavailable Infrastructure Service
description: A bounded period during which an infrastructure service is unavailable, felt from the outside as absence. A single component can fail with no outage following, and an outage can happen with no component failing at all: planned work or a missing input can take a service down just as surely.
sound_symbolism: The complete flowing form 'selu' begins with expected continuity, then hollow 'whe' opens the service's absence. The sound moves from availability into interruption while stopping short of naming a cause, destruction, or permanent end.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha seluwhe mosha teku nai.' (The outage period is short.) Name the service, affected place or participants, beginning, duration, and source separately where needed. Distinct from 'thonuki' (fail), 'whemoa' (empty), and any brief interruption that does not make a service unavailable.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Shared infrastructure must be discussable when it is absent as well as when it works, including unequal consequences and restoration priorities.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The word permits a neutral report of service loss while leaving notice, accountability, access, and harm open to explicit discussion.

tags.activity: cessation or inadequate provision of a technical function
tags.community: loss of a stated shared or infrastructure service for affected participants
tags.temporal: a bounded period of unavailability or interruption
